title: Database write latency delayed event consumers
failure_mechanism: Sustained database write latency reduced consumer throughput below incoming event volume, producing a growing but recoverable event backlog.
mitigation_summary: Processing recovered after database write latency returned to a stable range and consumers could drain the accumulated queue.
timeline_summary: Event processing slowed before the backlog became operationally visible; recovery required confirming write latency had stabilized before treating queue decline as meaningful.
narrative_safe: RelayOps event consumers remained available but processed work too slowly while a database write path degraded. The queue grew without a deployment trigger. The historical pattern is not equivalent to a consumer crash loop unless worker health is also degraded.
